medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presents with RUQ pain for 24 hours. Pain started after eating chips and has remained fairly constant since. She has had nausea and 6 episodes of emesis. This is her 4th episode of RUQ pain since her previous episodes resolved on their own in a few hours. Underwent RUQ US showing gallstones. Ate a cheeseburger 3 hours prior to presentation with worsening of her pain. Does not report fevers.
medical_history.family_history: RA in mother, acute cholecystitis in grandmother

physical_examination.general_examination: VS T 98.0 BP 98/65 HR 82 RR 18 O2Sat 95%@RA. Patient is alert and oriented, no acute distress. Abdomen is soft, non-distended, slightly tender to palpation.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Not specified. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs/gallops/rubs. Abdominal exam shows tenderness.
